regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Adjuvant

trial_data.os: 51.1 months for capecitabine vs. 36.4 months for observation (per-protocol analysis)
trial_data.hr: 0.75 (95% CI, 0.58-0.97; P=0.028) for OS in per-protocol analysis

regimens.notes: Adjuvant therapy up to 6 months. Based on the phase III BILCAP study showing improved overall survival in the per-protocol analysis. The intent-to-treat analysis did not reach statistical significance. [2]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Adjuvant
regimens.notes: This regimen is commonly followed by chemoradiation in instances where the combination of chemotherapy and chemoradiation is recommended. Limited clinical trial data; extrapolated from advanced disease settings. [3]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Adjuvant
regimens.notes: If a patient is ineligible for cisplatin, carboplatin may be used. Extrapolated from metastatic setting data. [4]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Adjuvant
regimens.notes: Single-agent fluoropyrimidine option. Limited data in adjuvant setting. [250]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Adjuvant
regimens.notes: Single-agent gemcitabine option. Phase III trials did not show significant benefit in adjuvant setting. [245,246]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Adjuvant or Unresectable
regimens.notes: Used as a radiosensitizer with concurrent radiation therapy. Limited clinical trial data; participation in clinical trials encouraged. [260,265]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Adjuvant or Unresectable
regimens.notes: Used as a radiosensitizer with concurrent radiation therapy. Limited clinical trial data. [316,317]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line for unresectable or metastatic disease

trial_data.os: 12.9 months for durvalumab vs. 11.3 months for placebo

regimens.notes: First-line preferred regimen. If cisplatin ineligible, carboplatin may be used. Also recommended for recurrent disease >6 months after surgery and adjuvant therapy. Monitor for immune-related adverse events. [5]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line for unresectable or metastatic disease

trial_data.os: 12.7 months for pembrolizumab vs. 10.9 months for placebo
trial_data.pfs: HR 0.86 (95% CI, 0.75-1.00; P=0.023) at first interim analysis

regimens.notes: First-line preferred regimen. Pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV pembrolizumab. If cisplatin ineligible, carboplatin may be used. [6]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line for unresectable or metastatic disease

trial_data.os: 11.7 months for cisplatin/gemcitabine vs. 8.1 months for gemcitabine alone

regimens.notes: Standard first-line chemotherapy. If cisplatin ineligible, carboplatin may be used. [7]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: Based on phase II trial data in cholangiocarcinoma. [301]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: Based on phase II trial data. [297,298,299,300]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: Based on phase III non-inferiority trial compared to GEMOX. [307]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: Based on phase II data. Also a preferred subsequent-line option. [303,304,305,306]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: Based on phase II trial data. [292,293,294,295]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: Single-agent option based on phase II data. [297,298,299,300]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: Single-agent option based on historical data. [270,277]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: Single-agent option based on phase III data (ABC-02). [189]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease

trial_data.os: 6.2 months for FOLFOX vs. 5.3 months for active symptom control

regimens.notes: Preferred subsequent-line regimen after progression on gemcitabine-based chemotherapy. [8]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: Subsequent-line option based on phase II data. [9,281,282]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease

trial_data.pfs: 4.2 months for liposomal irinotecan vs. 1.7 months for fluorouracil/leucovorin

regimens.notes: Subsequent-line option for patients with disease progression on gemcitabine and cisplatin. Based on pooled analysis of NIFTY and NALIRICC trials. [10,11,12]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease

trial_data.os: 5.3 months for regorafenib vs. 5.1 months for placebo

regimens.notes: Subsequent-line option for patients with chemotherapy-refractory disease. [14,400]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: Subsequent-line option for patients without prior checkpoint inhibitor therapy. Nivolumab and hyaluronidase-nvhy subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV nivolumab. [15,343]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line or subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For NTRK gene fusion-positive tumors. FDA-approved for tumor-agnostic indication. Response rates of 57-75% in pre-treated tumors. [16,17]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line or subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For NTRK gene fusion-positive tumors. FDA-approved for tumor-agnostic indication. Should not be used if progression on prior NTRK inhibitors. [18]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line or subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For NTRK gene fusion-positive tumors. Option if progression on prior TRK inhibitors. [19]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line or subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For MSI-H/dMMR tumors. Also for TMB-H tumors in subsequent-line. Pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV pembrolizumab. Limited data in first-line setting. [20,21,22,23]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For MSI-H/dMMR tumors that have progressed on prior treatment and have no satisfactory alternatives. Not for use after prior checkpoint inhibitor therapy. [29]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line or subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease

trial_data.os: 15.0 months for nivolumab/ipilimumab vs. 14.6 months for nivolumab in tTMB-H tumors

regimens.notes: For TMB-H tumors. In subsequent-line, for patients with disease refractory to standard therapies or no standard options. [24,342]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For TMB-H tumors in subsequent-line. Pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV pembrolizumab. [30,332]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For BRAF V600E-mutated tumors. FDA-approved for tumor-agnostic indication. [31,32,345,349]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For HER2-positive tumors (IHC3+). Antibody-drug conjugate. [33,374,375]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For HER2-positive tumors. Dual HER2 blockade. [34,377,378]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For HER2-positive tumors. Oral tyrosine kinase inhibitor with trastuzumab. [35,380]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For HER2-positive tumors (IHC3+). Bispecific antibody. [36,381]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line or subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For RET gene fusion-positive tumors. One patient with CCA had an objective response. [26,398]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: First-line or subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For RET gene fusion-positive tumors. Limited data in CCA. [25,391]

regimens.site: Biliary tract cancers (gallbladder cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma)
regimens.setting: Subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For KRAS G12C mutation-positive tumors. Oral KRAS inhibitor. [37,386]

regimens.site: Intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma or extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma with FGFR2 fusions/rearrangements
regimens.setting: Subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For FGFR2 fusion/rearrangement-positive CCA. Preferred over erdafitinib. [38,352]

regimens.site: Intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma or extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma with FGFR2 fusions/rearrangements
regimens.setting: Subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For FGFR2 fusion/rearrangement-positive CCA. Preferred over erdafitinib. [39,353,354]

regimens.site: Intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma or extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma with FGFR fusions/rearrangements
regimens.setting: Subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For FGFR-altered tumors. Less preferred than futibatinib or pemigatinib. Data from smaller trial. [40,355]

regimens.site: Intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma or extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma with IDH1 mutations
regimens.setting: Subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease

trial_data.os: 10.3 months for ivosidenib vs. 5.1 months for placebo after crossover

regimens.notes: For IDH1-mutant CCA. Category 1 recommendation based on phase III trial. [41,42,360,361]

regimens.site: Cholangiocarcinoma with NRG1 gene fusions
regimens.setting: First-line or subsequent-line for unresectable or metastatic disease
regimens.notes: For NRG1 fusion-positive CCA. Bispecific antibody. [27,28,36,27,28]
